title: Getting started with cryptRopen
text: cryptRopen pseudonymizes variables inside data frames sensitive values (identifiers, emails, names) can shared stored derivative form without exposing originals. pseudonymization deterministic: input value paired key always produces output, joins across tables remain possible pseudonymized columns. side table — correspondence table — keeps mapping original pseudonymized values party holds key. Typical use cases: preparing extract analytics team see customer identifiers; sharing dataset external partner; feeding downstream pipeline keeping original identifiers control.

title: Getting started with cryptRopen
text: crypt package name historical. underlying transformation salted hash, reversible encryption: normalized version input value concatenated user-provided salt (key) hashed digest::digest(). default algorithm "md5". algorithm supported digest::digest() ("sha1", "sha256", "sha512", …) accepted via algorithm parameter — see Algorithm choice . Holding output let anyone recover input. correspondence table (rainbow-table-style attack weak key) . Consequence: cryptRopen suitable pseudonymization regulations GDPR, confidentiality-bearing encryption.

title: Getting started with cryptRopen
text: Pseudonymization one tool among several. Reach different approach : need recover original value later. salted hash one-way. workflow must un-pseudonymize point, use symmetric encryption (e.g. safer sodium packages) manage key separately. need formal anonymization, pseudonymization. regulations GDPR, pseudonymized data still personal data — correspondence table de facto re-identification key. irreversible anonymization, consider differential privacy, k-anonymity, aggregation. want hide value distributions. deterministic hash preserves frequency profile original column. attacker knows rough distribution input (e.g. certain customer IDs occur often) can still spot frequent hashes. Add random padding switch format-preserving encryption matters threat model. need integrity guarantees data, just identifiers. Hashes computed per cell, whole row file. detect tampering dataset level — pair separate signature scheme need .

title: Getting started with cryptRopen
text: package delegates actual hashing digest::digest(), every algorithm function available via algorithm parameter. trade-offs know: "md5" (default) — fast short (32 hex chars). Cryptographic collisions exist theoretical constructions negligible practical dataset scales pseudonymization. Recommended unless specific reason step . "sha1" — 40 hex chars, deprecated formal cryptography still acceptable non-adversarial pseudonymization. Rarely better choice MD5 SHA-256. "sha256" — 64 hex chars, modern conservative default want safer side. Slower MD5 difference typically 1 % total runtime real pseudonymization job dominated /O. "sha512" — 128 hex chars, useful downstream systems happen expect 512-bit hash. output twice long practical security gain context. Whatever pick, lock run keep encryption_key across runs need join: changing either breaks deterministic property tables stop joining pseudonymized columns.

title: Getting started with cryptRopen
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("danielrak/cryptRopen") library(cryptRopen)

title: Getting started with cryptRopen
text: crypt_vector() lowest-level entry point. takes vector, key, hash algorithm, returns character vector length. NA empty strings stay NA. Input uppercased, trimmed, stripped internal spaces hashing, "Alice", "alice " " ALICE " collapse hash. Determinism NA / empty-string rules:
code: crypt_vector(   vector = c("Alice", "Bob", "Charlie"),   key = "my-secret-salt",   algo = "md5" ) #> [1] "450AC498E2781A99FCEE98F8D460A87E" "88C40ACBB5A8351455503E2EB8A44089" #> [3] "1EEAF5D958D4AF3BA58900DDB131BB18" # Same value + same key => same hash, every time. identical(   crypt_vector("Alice", key = "k", algo = "md5"),   crypt_vector("alice ", key = "k", algo = "md5") ) #> [1] TRUE  # NAs and empty strings are preserved. crypt_vector(c("Alice", NA, "", "  "), key = "k", algo = "md5") #> [1] "7A9C2C3ACBB6E4B93066B2B66501EB10" NA                                 #> [3] NA                                 NA

what: Hashing one or several columns of a data frame
title: Getting started with cryptRopen
text: crypt_data() operates data frame returns new data frame requested columns replaced hashed counterparts (suffix _crypt). Original columns can optionally dropped (vars_to_remove). correspondence table returned alongside data frame; stored inside package-private environment retrieved via get_correspondence_tables(): Tables accumulate across calls session. Pass character vector names pull ones need: Pseudonymising multiple columns matter extending vars_to_encrypt:
code: head(mtcars, 3) #>                mpg cyl disp  hp drat    wt  qsec vs am gear carb #> Mazda RX4     21.0   6  160 110 3.90 2.620 16.46  0  1    4    4 #> Mazda RX4 Wag 21.0   6  160 110 3.90 2.875 17.02  0  1    4    4 #> Datsun 710    22.8   4  108  93 3.85 2.320 18.61  1  1    4    1  encrypted <- crypt_data(   loaded_dataset            = head(mtcars, 3),   vars_to_encrypt           = "mpg",   vars_to_remove            = "cyl",   encryption_key            = "demo-key",   algorithm                 = "md5",   correspondence_table      = TRUE,   correspondence_table_label = "demo" ) encrypted #>                                      mpg_crypt disp  hp drat    wt  qsec vs am #> Mazda RX4     C33FDD39F6EAADF6C5E135AD5083E3D6  160 110 3.90 2.620 16.46  0  1 #> Mazda RX4 Wag C33FDD39F6EAADF6C5E135AD5083E3D6  160 110 3.90 2.875 17.02  0  1 #> Datsun 710    7E7E52C52B1C3686F553D1E4AC6A8207  108  93 3.85 2.320 18.61  1  1 #>               gear carb #> Mazda RX4        4    4 #> Mazda RX4 Wag    4    4 #> Datsun 710       4    1 tcs <- get_correspondence_tables() names(tcs) #> [1] "tc_crypt_demo" tcs$tc_crypt_demo #>                mpg                        mpg_crypt #> Mazda RX4     21.0 C33FDD39F6EAADF6C5E135AD5083E3D6 #> Mazda RX4 Wag 21.0 C33FDD39F6EAADF6C5E135AD5083E3D6 #> Datsun 710    22.8 7E7E52C52B1C3686F553D1E4AC6A8207 get_correspondence_tables(names = "tc_crypt_demo") #> $tc_crypt_demo #>                mpg                        mpg_crypt #> Mazda RX4     21.0 C33FDD39F6EAADF6C5E135AD5083E3D6 #> Mazda RX4 Wag 21.0 C33FDD39F6EAADF6C5E135AD5083E3D6 #> Datsun 710    22.8 7E7E52C52B1C3686F553D1E4AC6A8207 crypt_data(   loaded_dataset            = head(mtcars, 3),   vars_to_encrypt           = c("mpg", "hp"),   encryption_key            = "demo-key",   correspondence_table      = TRUE,   correspondence_table_label = "demo_multi" ) #>                                      mpg_crypt                         hp_crypt #> Mazda RX4     C33FDD39F6EAADF6C5E135AD5083E3D6 7D10FEDBB84390262D97BCD0956D8762 #> Mazda RX4 Wag C33FDD39F6EAADF6C5E135AD5083E3D6 7D10FEDBB84390262D97BCD0956D8762 #> Datsun 710    7E7E52C52B1C3686F553D1E4AC6A8207 BB8BE843A95B4E5921897169087511EC #>               cyl disp drat    wt  qsec vs am gear carb #> Mazda RX4       6  160 3.90 2.620 16.46  0  1    4    4 #> Mazda RX4 Wag   6  160 3.90 2.875 17.02  0  1    4    4 #> Datsun 710      4  108 3.85 2.320 18.61  1  1    4    1

what: When you need to batch multiple files
title: Getting started with cryptRopen
text: crypt_data() right tool hold one data frame memory want encrypted version . industrialized workflow — several input files specified Excel mask, outputs written disk, parallel execution, recap log — see vignette("crypt_r-workflow").

title: Getting started with cryptRopen
text: ?crypt_vector, ?crypt_data, ?crypt_r — three public entry points. ?get_correspondence_tables — retrieval correspondence tables stored package-private environment. NEWS.md — full change history.

title: Industrialized workflow with crypt_r()
text: crypt_r() driven single Excel mask describes encrypt . Required columns: Duplicated encrypted_file values automatically disambiguated DUPL<n>_ prefix, output paths stay unique.

title: Industrialized workflow with crypt_r()
text: mask row whose vars_to_encrypt cell empty (blank, NA, whitespace-) legitimate. means “process file, encrypting nothing”: input file written output_path re-encoded output format implied encrypted_file (e.g. csv → csv, parquet → parquet); vars_to_remove still applied, empty-vars row non-empty vars_to_remove clean “purge columns copy” instruction; _crypt columns emitted, tc_*.parquet written intermediate_path, recap log records success = TRUE tc_name = NA row. crypt_data() support : calling without anything encrypt treated misuse raises explicit error pointing dplyr / rio plain column dropping format conversion.

title: Industrialized workflow with crypt_r()
text: example uses 10-row CSV fixture shipped package (inst/extdata/persons.csv). Every write happens inside tempdir(), example leaves nothing behind machine. Build minimal mask: one row, pseudonymising email dropping joined_date. Dispatch job. crypt_r() returns immediately, workers finished; value cryptR_job handle.
code: input_file    <- system.file("extdata", "persons.csv", package = "cryptRopen") input_folder  <- dirname(input_file)  work_dir           <- tempfile("cryptR_vignette_") dir.create(work_dir) mask_dir           <- file.path(work_dir, "mask") output_dir         <- file.path(work_dir, "output") intermediate_dir   <- file.path(work_dir, "intermediate") dir.create(mask_dir) dir.create(output_dir) dir.create(intermediate_dir) mask <- data.frame(   folder_path     = input_folder,   file            = basename(input_file),   encrypted_file  = "persons_crypt.csv",   vars_to_encrypt = "email",   vars_to_remove  = "joined_date",   to_encrypt      = "X",   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) writexl::write_xlsx(mask, file.path(mask_dir, "mask.xlsx")) job <- crypt_r(   mask_folder_path  = mask_dir,   mask_file         = "mask.xlsx",   output_path       = output_dir,   intermediate_path = intermediate_dir,   encryption_key    = "vignette-key",   algorithm         = "md5",   n_workers         = 1L ) class(job) #> [1] "cryptR_job"

title: Industrialized workflow with crypt_r()
text: Three orthogonal views available time run.

title: Industrialized workflow with crypt_r()
text: Columns: encrypted_file, state (running / done / failed), error_message, plus start_time, end_time, duration_sec, n_rows_processed. NA task resolves.
code: cryptR_wait(job) cryptR_status(job) #>                      encrypted_file state error_message          start_time #> persons_crypt.csv persons_crypt.csv  done          <NA> 2026-06-03 21:22:17 #>                              end_time duration_sec n_rows_processed #> persons_crypt.csv 2026-06-03 21:22:18     0.728658               10

title: Industrialized workflow with crypt_r()
text: Returns compact object task counts state, elapsed seconds, active workers, total rows processed, output path, full status data frame $status.
code: summary(job) #> <cryptR_job summary> #>   tasks        : 1 #>     running    : 0 #>     done       : 1 #>     failed     : 0 #>   workers      : 1 #>   elapsed      : 1.20 s #>   rows total   : 10 #>   output_path  : /tmp/RtmpjfDzwk/cryptR_vignette_1d9c6290101f/output #>   log_written  : FALSE

title: Industrialized workflow with crypt_r()
text: One row per filtered mask row expected output_file_path, live exists flag, size_bytes / sha256 worker recorded right writing output. Joins cleanly cryptR_status() encrypted_file.
code: cryptR_results(job) #>      encrypted_file #> 1 persons_crypt.csv #>                                                        output_file_path exists #> 1 /tmp/RtmpjfDzwk/cryptR_vignette_1d9c6290101f/output/persons_crypt.csv   TRUE #>   size_bytes                                                           sha256 #> 1        614 b486135ce0e87616e4b447ce9bc8185acdc8c286dc310c8de5a98026701037e9 #>   success error_message #> 1    TRUE          <NA>

title: Industrialized workflow with crypt_r()
text: cryptR_collect() waits tasks resolve (called cryptR_wait() already), re-publishes correspondence tables produced inside mirai daemons back session’s environment, writes recap log, tears daemons crypt_r() created run. correspondence table now visible parent session: recap log xlsx file whose name embeds run timestamp: auto-watcher (based later::later()) writes log soon last mirai task resolves, manual cryptR_collect() call strictly required log appear — just becomes reliable synchronisation point.
code: job <- cryptR_collect(job) tcs <- get_correspondence_tables() names(tcs) #> [1] "tc_persons_crypt" head(tcs$tc_persons_crypt) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   email         email_crypt                      #>   <chr>         <chr>                            #> 1 a@example.com AE0B96F635D0ECC0335AF23BF4AB5C65 #> 2 b@example.com 741FD2483C97A3D557657C01744CB69A #> 3 c@example.com A8443E50BC83A0B19186165AD010811E #> 4 d@example.com 2FE239E57F1C4D022160CD162AD0F88B #> 5 e@example.com 1635B322A0FB470E3A2A5858748B67FD #> 6 f@example.com EB7A394EA07ADFB7C3C76987D60DF855 list.files(output_dir, pattern = "^log_crypt_r_.*\\.xlsx$") #> [1] "log_crypt_r_20260603_212218.xlsx"

title: Industrialized workflow with crypt_r()
text: streaming mode input read chunks arrow Scanner written incrementally, keeping memory usage bounded regardless input size. chunk_size argument controls row count per chunk; default (1e6) reasonable trade-memory footprint per-chunk overhead.

title: Industrialized workflow with crypt_r()
text: mirai daemons active default profile crypt_r() called, spawns n_workers flags job automatic teardown cryptR_collect(). daemons already running, crypt_r() reuses cryptR_collect() leaves alone — useful wrap several crypt_r() calls inside broader parallel session manage .

title: Industrialized workflow with crypt_r()
text: failure one mask row never interrupts others. failure captured corresponding mirai task surfaces : state == "failed" cryptR_status(), non-NA error_message column, success = FALSE xlsx recap log. Invalid output_path intermediate_path — paths don’t exist, aren’t scalar non-empty character — caught task dispatched, clear error.

title: Industrialized workflow with crypt_r()
text: Nothing written outside tempdir(), example cleans R session ends. can also drop folder explicitly:
code: unlink(work_dir, recursive = TRUE)

title: Industrialized workflow with crypt_r()
text: ?crypt_r, ?cryptR_status, ?cryptR_wait, ?cryptR_collect, ?cryptR_results, ?summary.cryptR_job. vignette("cryptRopen") -session workflow crypt_data(). NEWS.md full change history.

what: Industrialized Pseudonymization of Variables Across Datasets
title: Industrialized Pseudonymization of Variables Across Datasets
text: cryptRopen pseudonymizes variables one many datasets, driven single Excel mask lists, per input file, columns hash drop. transformation salted hash (MD5 default; algorithm supported digest::digest() works), means: input value paired key always yields hash. Joins across tables pseudonymized columns stay possible. hash one-way: holding output let anyone recover input. correspondence table (kept party owns key) . Suitable GDPR-style pseudonymization. suitable reversible encryption scheme confidentiality-bearing data.

title: Industrialized Pseudonymization of Variables Across Datasets
text: CRAN: Development version GitHub:
code: install.packages("cryptRopen") # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("danielrak/cryptRopen")

title: Industrialized Pseudonymization of Variables Across Datasets
text: Pseudonymize one data frame already memory. correspondence table kept package-private environment retrieved get_correspondence_tables():
code: library(cryptRopen)  pseudonymized <- crypt_data(   loaded_dataset             = head(mtcars, 3),   vars_to_encrypt            = "mpg",   vars_to_remove             = "cyl",   encryption_key             = "demo-key",   correspondence_table       = TRUE,   correspondence_table_label = "demo" ) pseudonymized #>                                      mpg_crypt disp  hp drat    wt  qsec vs am #> Mazda RX4     C33FDD39F6EAADF6C5E135AD5083E3D6  160 110 3.90 2.620 16.46  0  1 #> Mazda RX4 Wag C33FDD39F6EAADF6C5E135AD5083E3D6  160 110 3.90 2.875 17.02  0  1 #> Datsun 710    7E7E52C52B1C3686F553D1E4AC6A8207  108  93 3.85 2.320 18.61  1  1 #>               gear carb #> Mazda RX4        4    4 #> Mazda RX4 Wag    4    4 #> Datsun 710       4    1  get_correspondence_tables()$tc_crypt_demo #>                mpg                        mpg_crypt #> Mazda RX4     21.0 C33FDD39F6EAADF6C5E135AD5083E3D6 #> Mazda RX4 Wag 21.0 C33FDD39F6EAADF6C5E135AD5083E3D6 #> Datsun 710    22.8 7E7E52C52B1C3686F553D1E4AC6A8207

title: Industrialized Pseudonymization of Variables Across Datasets
text: crypt_r() reads Excel mask describing N input files, dispatches one mirai::mirai() task per row (parallel, non-blocking), writes pseudonymized files recap xlsx log disk, returns cryptR_job handle. Inspect cryptR_status() / summary() / cryptR_results() finalize cryptR_collect(). row mask declares vars_to_encrypt, different input files can entirely different column names — mask contract, single schema. Large parquet / CSV inputs automatically routed streaming engine (arrow::open_dataset() + chunked write) memory usage stays bounded regardless input size.
code: job <- crypt_r(   mask_folder_path  = "path/to/mask",   mask_file         = "mask.xlsx",   output_path       = "path/to/output",   intermediate_path = "path/to/intermediate",   encryption_key    = "your-salt",   algorithm         = "md5" )  cryptR_status(job) job <- cryptR_collect(job)

title: Industrialized Pseudonymization of Variables Across Datasets
text: vignette("cryptRopen") — getting started: -session workflow crypt_vector() / crypt_data(), difference pseudonymization encryption, pick algorithm. vignette("crypt_r-workflow") — full walkthrough mask-driven crypt_r() workflow: Excel mask layout, async inspection, streaming, daemons ownership, recap log. NEWS.md — change history.

what: Finalize an asynchronous crypt_r() job — cryptR_collect
title: Finalize an asynchronous crypt_r() job — cryptR_collect
text: Waits tasks resolve (see cryptR_wait()), extracts per-row results, re-publishes correspondence tables parent's .cryptRopen_env (get_correspondence_tables() sees async run), writes recap log log_crypt_r_<timestamp>.xlsx job$output_path, — crypt_r() created mirai daemons (daemons_owned_by_job = TRUE) — tears .

title: Finalize an asynchronous crypt_r() job — cryptR_collect
code: cryptR_collect(job, timeout = Inf, poll_interval = 0.1)

title: Finalize an asynchronous crypt_r() job — cryptR_collect
text: job cryptR_job object. timeout Numeric. Maximum wait seconds. Defaults Inf. expiration, error class cryptR_timeout raised. poll_interval Numeric. Polling period seconds. Defaults 0.1.

title: Finalize an asynchronous crypt_r() job — cryptR_collect
text: invisible(job) — log_written = TRUE , applicable, daemons_torn_down = TRUE. modifications applied returned object ; S3 objects mutable place R, callers want updated flags must capture return value (job <- cryptR_collect(job)).

title: Finalize an asynchronous crypt_r() job — cryptR_collect
text: Idempotent two independent axes: job$log_written guards log-writing + TC re-injection block, manual cryptR_collect() called auto watcher already run -op — duplicate xlsx, TCs stored twice. job$daemons_torn_down guards mirai::daemons(0) call second collect attempt double teardown. daemons set externally crypt_r() called, daemons_owned_by_job FALSE teardown never attempted — user retains control daemons.

title: Finalize an asynchronous crypt_r() job — cryptR_collect
code: # \donttest{ # Build a tiny job (see ?crypt_r for a more detailed walkthrough) work_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "cryptR_collect_example") mask_dir <- file.path(work_dir, "mask") out_dir  <- file.path(work_dir, "output") int_dir  <- file.path(work_dir, "intermediate") for (d in c(mask_dir, out_dir, int_dir)) {   dir.create(d, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE) }  input_file <- system.file("extdata", "persons.csv", package = "cryptRopen") mask <- data.frame(   folder_path     = dirname(input_file),   file            = basename(input_file),   encrypted_file  = "persons_crypt.csv",   vars_to_encrypt = "email",   vars_to_remove  = NA,   to_encrypt      = "X",   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) writexl::write_xlsx(mask, file.path(mask_dir, "mask.xlsx"))  job <- crypt_r(   mask_folder_path  = mask_dir,   mask_file         = "mask.xlsx",   output_path       = out_dir,   intermediate_path = int_dir,   encryption_key    = "demo-key",   n_workers         = 1L ) job <- cryptR_collect(job)  cryptR_status(job) #>                      encrypted_file state error_message          start_time #> persons_crypt.csv persons_crypt.csv  done          <NA> 2026-06-03 21:22:05 #>                              end_time duration_sec n_rows_processed #> persons_crypt.csv 2026-06-03 21:22:06    0.7252164               10 unlink(work_dir, recursive = TRUE) # }

what: Per-output disk-oriented view of an async crypt_r() job — cryptR_results
title: Per-output disk-oriented view of an async crypt_r() job — cryptR_results
text: Returns one row per filtered mask row () expected output file path, (b) whether currently exists disk, (c) size/hash worker captured right writing . Companion cryptR_status() — two views orthogonal join cleanly encrypted_file.

title: Per-output disk-oriented view of an async crypt_r() job — cryptR_results
code: cryptR_results(job)

title: Per-output disk-oriented view of an async crypt_r() job — cryptR_results
text: job cryptR_job object, returned crypt_r().

title: Per-output disk-oriented view of an async crypt_r() job — cryptR_results
text: data.frame columns: encrypted_file (character): row identifier (dedup'd file name). output_file_path (character): full path output / written. exists (logical): result file.exists(output_file_path) call time. size_bytes (numeric): size recorded worker post-write, NA task produced payload yet. sha256 (character): sha256 output worker-completion time, NA otherwise. success (logical): whether engine reported clean end. error_message (character): concatenated engine errors, NA success.

title: Per-output disk-oriented view of an async crypt_r() job — cryptR_results
text: Running tasks contribute row exists = FALSE NA size_bytes / sha256. Tasks failed reaching final export contribute row success = FALSE, exists reflecting live file state (may still FALSE engine short-circuited), error_message set. size_bytes / sha256 values ones engine recorded inside worker. output file modified removed call cryptR_results(), columns reflect — describe produced, currently disk. Use file.info() / digest::digest() live measurements needed.

title: Per-output disk-oriented view of an async crypt_r() job — cryptR_results
code: # \donttest{ # Build a tiny job (see ?crypt_r for a more detailed walkthrough) work_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "cryptR_results_example") mask_dir <- file.path(work_dir, "mask") out_dir  <- file.path(work_dir, "output") int_dir  <- file.path(work_dir, "intermediate") for (d in c(mask_dir, out_dir, int_dir)) {   dir.create(d, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE) }  input_file <- system.file("extdata", "persons.csv", package = "cryptRopen") mask <- data.frame(   folder_path     = dirname(input_file),   file            = basename(input_file),   encrypted_file  = "persons_crypt.csv",   vars_to_encrypt = "email",   vars_to_remove  = NA,   to_encrypt      = "X",   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) writexl::write_xlsx(mask, file.path(mask_dir, "mask.xlsx"))  job <- crypt_r(   mask_folder_path  = mask_dir,   mask_file         = "mask.xlsx",   output_path       = out_dir,   intermediate_path = int_dir,   encryption_key    = "demo-key",   n_workers         = 1L ) job <- cryptR_collect(job) cryptR_results(job) #>      encrypted_file #> 1 persons_crypt.csv #>                                                  output_file_path exists #> 1 /tmp/Rtmp7huNLr/cryptR_results_example/output/persons_crypt.csv   TRUE #>   size_bytes                                                           sha256 #> 1        738 9dceb0aa9241af79f603dad25444dfaab3e586837040544192296f349c01e51d #>   success error_message #> 1    TRUE          <NA>  unlink(work_dir, recursive = TRUE) # }

what: Inspect the state of an asynchronous crypt_r() job — cryptR_status
title: Inspect the state of an asynchronous crypt_r() job — cryptR_status
text: Returns one-row-per-task snapshot job's progress. result taken time call: task resolved two successive calls flip running done/failed, function side effects (log writing, waiting).

title: Inspect the state of an asynchronous crypt_r() job — cryptR_status
code: cryptR_status(job)

title: Inspect the state of an asynchronous crypt_r() job — cryptR_status
text: job cryptR_job object, returned crypt_r().

title: Inspect the state of an asynchronous crypt_r() job — cryptR_status
text: data.frame columns encrypted_file (character), state (factor: running / done / failed), error_message (character, NA unless state == "failed"), start_time / end_time (POSIXct, NA task resolves), duration_sec (numeric seconds, NA task resolves), n_rows_processed (integer, NA task resolves engine reports row count).

title: Inspect the state of an asynchronous crypt_r() job — cryptR_status
text: snapshot also carries per-row metrics (start_time, end_time, duration_sec, n_rows_processed) resolved tasks — populated task payload shipped back engines (.make_row_result()). Metrics NA tasks still running errored producing payload; lets monitor run -flight without reading log_crypt_r_*.xlsx file disk.

title: Inspect the state of an asynchronous crypt_r() job — cryptR_status
code: # \donttest{ # Build a tiny job (see ?crypt_r for a more detailed walkthrough) work_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "cryptR_status_example") mask_dir <- file.path(work_dir, "mask") out_dir  <- file.path(work_dir, "output") int_dir  <- file.path(work_dir, "intermediate") for (d in c(mask_dir, out_dir, int_dir)) {   dir.create(d, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE) }  input_file <- system.file("extdata", "persons.csv", package = "cryptRopen") mask <- data.frame(   folder_path     = dirname(input_file),   file            = basename(input_file),   encrypted_file  = "persons_crypt.csv",   vars_to_encrypt = "email",   vars_to_remove  = NA,   to_encrypt      = "X",   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) writexl::write_xlsx(mask, file.path(mask_dir, "mask.xlsx"))  job <- crypt_r(   mask_folder_path  = mask_dir,   mask_file         = "mask.xlsx",   output_path       = out_dir,   intermediate_path = int_dir,   encryption_key    = "demo-key",   n_workers         = 1L ) cryptR_status(job) #>                      encrypted_file   state error_message start_time end_time #> persons_crypt.csv persons_crypt.csv running          <NA>       <NA>     <NA> #>                   duration_sec n_rows_processed #> persons_crypt.csv           NA               NA  cryptR_collect(job) unlink(work_dir, recursive = TRUE) # }

what: Block until an asynchronous crypt_r() job finishes — cryptR_wait
title: Block until an asynchronous crypt_r() job finishes — cryptR_wait
text: Polls every task job returns resolved (either done failed). failed task raise exception — state simply visible via cryptR_status().

title: Block until an asynchronous crypt_r() job finishes — cryptR_wait
code: cryptR_wait(job, timeout = Inf, poll_interval = 0.1)

title: Block until an asynchronous crypt_r() job finishes — cryptR_wait
text: job cryptR_job object. timeout Numeric. Maximum wait seconds. Defaults Inf. expiration, error class cryptR_timeout raised. poll_interval Numeric. Polling period seconds. Defaults 0.1.

title: Block until an asynchronous crypt_r() job finishes — cryptR_wait
text: invisible(job).

title: Block until an asynchronous crypt_r() job finishes — cryptR_wait
code: # \donttest{ # Build a tiny job (see ?crypt_r for a more detailed walkthrough) work_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "cryptR_wait_example") mask_dir <- file.path(work_dir, "mask") out_dir  <- file.path(work_dir, "output") int_dir  <- file.path(work_dir, "intermediate") for (d in c(mask_dir, out_dir, int_dir)) {   dir.create(d, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE) }  input_file <- system.file("extdata", "persons.csv", package = "cryptRopen") mask <- data.frame(   folder_path     = dirname(input_file),   file            = basename(input_file),   encrypted_file  = "persons_crypt.csv",   vars_to_encrypt = "email",   vars_to_remove  = NA,   to_encrypt      = "X",   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) writexl::write_xlsx(mask, file.path(mask_dir, "mask.xlsx"))  job <- crypt_r(   mask_folder_path  = mask_dir,   mask_file         = "mask.xlsx",   output_path       = out_dir,   intermediate_path = int_dir,   encryption_key    = "demo-key",   n_workers         = 1L ) cryptR_wait(job, timeout = 60)  cryptR_collect(job) unlink(work_dir, recursive = TRUE) # }

what: cryptRopen: Industrialized Pseudonymization of Variables Across Datasets — cryptRopen-package
title: cryptRopen: Industrialized Pseudonymization of Variables Across Datasets — cryptRopen-package
text: Deterministic, salted-hash pseudonymization one many datasets, driven single Excel mask lists, per input file, columns hash drop.

title: cryptRopen: Industrialized Pseudonymization of Variables Across Datasets — cryptRopen-package
text: Three entry points cover typical needs: crypt_vector() — hash one vector. crypt_data() — pseudonymize one data frame already memory; -session workflow. crypt_r() — pseudonymize N files described Excel mask, parallel, non-blocking; industrialized workflow. Inspect / wait / finalize returned job cryptR_status(), cryptR_wait(), cryptR_collect(), cryptR_results(), summary(). Use get_correspondence_tables() retrieve (original, hashed) mapping stored crypt_data() / crypt_r() runs.

title: cryptRopen: Industrialized Pseudonymization of Variables Across Datasets — cryptRopen-package
text: vignette("cryptRopen") — getting started. vignette("crypt_r-workflow") — industrialized walkthrough. NEWS.md — change history.

title: cryptRopen: Industrialized Pseudonymization of Variables Across Datasets — cryptRopen-package
text: Maintainer: Daniel Rakotomalala rakdanielh@gmail.com

what: Pseudonymize Variables in a Data Frame — crypt_data
title: Pseudonymize Variables in a Data Frame — crypt_data
text: -session version pseudonymization workflow: takes data frame, hashes requested columns user-provided salt, returns transformed data frame. hash deterministic one-way (see crypt_vector() details transformation).

title: Pseudonymize Variables in a Data Frame — crypt_data
code: crypt_data(   loaded_dataset,   vars_to_encrypt,   vars_to_remove = NULL,   encryption_key,   algorithm = "md5",   correspondence_table = TRUE,   correspondence_table_label = NULL )

title: Pseudonymize Variables in a Data Frame — crypt_data
text: loaded_dataset data frame. vars_to_encrypt Character vector column names hash. Must resolve least one non-empty entry trimming dropping NA / blank values; empty vector raises error. vars_to_remove Character vector column names drop output. NULL (default) keeps non-hashed columns. encryption_key Character scalar. salt prepended value hashing. See crypt_vector(). algorithm Character scalar. algorithm accepted algo argument digest::digest(). Defaults "md5". correspondence_table Logical scalar. TRUE (default), stores correspondence table package-private environment name tc_crypt_<correspondence_table_label>. Set FALSE skip side effect entirely. correspondence_table_label Character scalar. Required correspondence_table = TRUE. Used build storage name successive calls session overwrite .

title: Pseudonymize Variables in a Data Frame — crypt_data
text: data frame row order loaded_dataset, vars_to_encrypt columns replaced hashed counterparts (suffix _crypt), vars_to_remove columns dropped.

title: Pseudonymize Variables in a Data Frame — crypt_data
text: correspondence_table = TRUE, (original, hashed) pairs stored package-private environment name tc_crypt_<correspondence_table_label> retrieved via get_correspondence_tables(). file written; side effect replaces older behavior polluted globalenv(). crypt_data() fail-fast empty vars_to_encrypt: raises explicit error rather silently returning input unchanged. "drop columns / convert format" workflow batch files described Excel mask, use crypt_r() blank vars_to_encrypt cell — path legitimate. single -memory object, use dplyr::select() / rio::export() directly.

title: Pseudonymize Variables in a Data Frame — crypt_data
code: # Replace `mpg` with `mpg_crypt`, drop `cyl`, no correspondence # table side effect on this run. crypt_data(   loaded_dataset       = mtcars[1:5, ],   vars_to_encrypt      = "mpg",   vars_to_remove       = "cyl",   encryption_key       = "1234567",   algorithm            = "md5",   correspondence_table = FALSE ) #>                                          mpg_crypt disp  hp drat    wt  qsec vs #> Mazda RX4         CEA9D4F43791ACE22D90EDBF6CE405F5  160 110 3.90 2.620 16.46  0 #> Mazda RX4 Wag     CEA9D4F43791ACE22D90EDBF6CE405F5  160 110 3.90 2.875 17.02  0 #> Datsun 710        65080BE2078D5C5934F018F806E72E81  108  93 3.85 2.320 18.61  1 #> Hornet 4 Drive    18C5941010E3CDCB669F472DF3A1DD5E  258 110 3.08 3.215 19.44  1 #> Hornet Sportabout 1866EA523631A281BD9431915F7C407F  360 175 3.15 3.440 17.02  0 #>                   am gear carb #> Mazda RX4          1    4    4 #> Mazda RX4 Wag      1    4    4 #> Datsun 710         1    4    1 #> Hornet 4 Drive     0    3    1 #> Hornet Sportabout  0    3    2

what: Pseudonymize Variables Across Multiple Files via an Excel Mask — crypt_r
title: Pseudonymize Variables Across Multiple Files via an Excel Mask — crypt_r
text: high-level entry point batch / industrialized workflow. Reads single Excel mask describes, input file, columns hash drop; dispatches one mirai task per filtered mask row (parallel, non-blocking); returns immediately cryptR_job handle. job inspected cryptR_status() / summary() / cryptR_results(), blocked cryptR_wait(), finalized cryptR_collect() (writes recap xlsx log tears daemons applicable).

title: Pseudonymize Variables Across Multiple Files via an Excel Mask — crypt_r
code: crypt_r(   mask_folder_path,   mask_file,   output_path,   intermediate_path,   encryption_key,   algorithm = "md5",   correspondence_table = TRUE,   engine = c("auto", "in_memory", "streaming"),   chunk_size = 1000000L,   n_workers = NULL )

title: Pseudonymize Variables Across Multiple Files via an Excel Mask — crypt_r
text: mask_folder_path Character scalar. Directory containing Excel mask file. mask_file Character scalar. Name Excel mask file (extension). output_path Character scalar. Existing directory pseudonymized output files written. Validated -front. intermediate_path Character scalar. Existing directory correspondence-table parquets (tc_*.parquet) recap xlsx log written. Validated -front. encryption_key Character scalar. salt prepended value hashing. See crypt_vector() underlying transformation. algorithm Character scalar. algorithm accepted algo argument digest::digest(). Defaults "md5". correspondence_table Logical scalar. TRUE (default), per-input correspondence table tc_<stem>.parquet written intermediate_path also stored package-private environment (retrievable via get_correspondence_tables() cryptR_collect() re-injects post-run). engine One "auto", "in_memory", "streaming". Selects per-row processing engine. "auto" (default) "streaming" route parquet-/parquet-csv-/csv-streaming engines ('arrow' Scanner chunks, progressive write); mixed non-streamable endpoints (rds, xlsx, parquet→csv, csv→parquet) silently fall back "in_memory". "in_memory" always reads full input RAM. chunk_size Integer scalar. Number rows per chunk effective engine streaming. Ignored "in_memory". Defaults 1e6. n_workers Integer scalar NULL. Number mirai daemons spawn dispatch. NULL (default), crypt_r() picks min(parallel::detectCores() - 1, n_rows, 8L) (floored 1). daemons already running default profile crypt_r() called, n_workers ignored existing daemons reused — user retains control cryptR_collect() tear .

title: Pseudonymize Variables Across Multiple Files via an Excel Mask — crypt_r
text: cryptR_job object carrying one mirai task per filtered mask row. Inspect cryptR_status() / summary() / cryptR_results(); block cryptR_wait(); finalize cryptR_collect().

title: Pseudonymize Variables Across Multiple Files via an Excel Mask — crypt_r
text: per-row failure interrupt rows — failure captured corresponding task surfaces via cryptR_status(). Empty vars_to_encrypt cell. mask row whose vars_to_encrypt cell blank (empty, NA, whitespace-) legitimate means "process file, applying vars_to_remove , hashing nothing." output file written output_path re-encoded format implied encrypted_file; _crypt columns emitted, tc_*.parquet produced. recap log records success = TRUE tc_name = NA rows. crypt_data() support case — see documentation.

title: Pseudonymize Variables Across Multiple Files via an Excel Mask — crypt_r
code: # \donttest{ # Minimal end-to-end run using the persons.csv fixture shipped # with the package. Everything is written to tempdir() and cleaned # up at the end. work_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "crypt_r_example") mask_dir <- file.path(work_dir, "mask") out_dir  <- file.path(work_dir, "output") int_dir  <- file.path(work_dir, "intermediate") dir.create(mask_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE) dir.create(out_dir, showWarnings = FALSE) dir.create(int_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)  input_file <- system.file("extdata", "persons.csv", package = "cryptRopen") mask <- data.frame(   folder_path     = dirname(input_file),   file            = basename(input_file),   encrypted_file  = "persons_crypt.csv",   vars_to_encrypt = "email",   vars_to_remove  = NA,   to_encrypt      = "X",   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) writexl::write_xlsx(mask, file.path(mask_dir, "mask.xlsx"))  # n_workers = 1L keeps the example friendly to CRAN check policies # on parallelism in examples. job <- crypt_r(   mask_folder_path  = mask_dir,   mask_file         = "mask.xlsx",   output_path       = out_dir,   intermediate_path = int_dir,   encryption_key    = "demo-key",   n_workers         = 1L ) job <- cryptR_collect(job)  list.files(out_dir) #> [1] "inspect_persons_crypt.csv.xlsx"   "log_crypt_r_20260603_212212.xlsx" #> [3] "persons_crypt.csv"                unlink(work_dir, recursive = TRUE) # }

what: Hash a Vector with a Salted Pre-Image — crypt_vector
title: Hash a Vector with a Salted Pre-Image — crypt_vector
text: Returns hash length input vector. element first coerced character, upper-cased, trimmed, stripped internal whitespace, concatenated key hashed digest::digest(). transformation one-way deterministic: (value, key) pair always produces hash, original value recovered hash alone.

title: Hash a Vector with a Salted Pre-Image — crypt_vector
code: crypt_vector(vector, key, algo)

title: Hash a Vector with a Salted Pre-Image — crypt_vector
text: vector Atomic vector values hash. Non-character inputs coerced character normalization. key Character scalar. salt prepended value hashing. Choose value enough entropy rainbow-table attacks impractical. algo Character scalar. algorithm accepted algo argument digest::digest() ("md5", "sha1", "sha256", …).

title: Hash a Vector with a Salted Pre-Image — crypt_vector
text: Character vector upper-case hexadecimal hashes, length vector. NA every position input NA empty.

title: Hash a Vector with a Salted Pre-Image — crypt_vector
text: NA values empty strings preserved NA output — hashed.

title: Hash a Vector with a Salted Pre-Image — crypt_vector
code: # Three IDs, a missing value, an empty string. The non-empty values # come back as upper-case MD5 hashes; `NA` and "" stay `NA`. crypt_vector(   vector = c("1234", "5678", "9101112", NA, ""),   key = "123456",   algo = "md5" ) #> [1] "B38E2BF274239FF5DD2B45EE9AE099C9" "D71B95E05E2208136FEA6F9052FD6F9B" #> [3] "3F9A1DBCC3C016DE3DC05F247F23D01D" NA                                 #> [5] NA                                  # Determinism: same value + same key + same algo => same hash. identical(   crypt_vector("Alice",  key = "k", algo = "md5"),   crypt_vector("alice ", key = "k", algo = "md5") ) #> [1] TRUE

what: Retrieve Correspondence Tables from the Current Session — get_correspondence_tables
title: Retrieve Correspondence Tables from the Current Session — get_correspondence_tables
text: Correspondence tables (original, hashed) mappings built call crypt_data() crypt_r(). stored package-private environment accumulates across successive calls within R session cleared session ends.

title: Retrieve Correspondence Tables from the Current Session — get_correspondence_tables
code: get_correspondence_tables(names = NULL)

title: Retrieve Correspondence Tables from the Current Session — get_correspondence_tables
text: names Optional character vector table names return. NULL (default), every table currently stored returned. vector passed, result preserves requested order; names absent store appear NULL entries caller can detect misses via .null(result[[name]]).

title: Retrieve Correspondence Tables from the Current Session — get_correspondence_tables
text: named list data frames / tibbles. Names follow convention tc_crypt_<label> crypt_data() tc_<encrypted_file_without_extension> crypt_r(). empty list correspondence table produced yet session.

title: Retrieve Correspondence Tables from the Current Session — get_correspondence_tables
code: # Run crypt_data() once to populate the store, then retrieve. crypt_data(   loaded_dataset             = mtcars[1:5, ],   vars_to_encrypt            = "mpg",   encryption_key             = "1234567",   correspondence_table       = TRUE,   correspondence_table_label = "demo" ) #>                                          mpg_crypt cyl disp  hp drat    wt #> Mazda RX4         CEA9D4F43791ACE22D90EDBF6CE405F5   6  160 110 3.90 2.620 #> Mazda RX4 Wag     CEA9D4F43791ACE22D90EDBF6CE405F5   6  160 110 3.90 2.875 #> Datsun 710        65080BE2078D5C5934F018F806E72E81   4  108  93 3.85 2.320 #> Hornet 4 Drive    18C5941010E3CDCB669F472DF3A1DD5E   6  258 110 3.08 3.215 #> Hornet Sportabout 1866EA523631A281BD9431915F7C407F   8  360 175 3.15 3.440 #>                    qsec vs am gear carb #> Mazda RX4         16.46  0  1    4    4 #> Mazda RX4 Wag     17.02  0  1    4    4 #> Datsun 710        18.61  1  1    4    1 #> Hornet 4 Drive    19.44  1  0    3    1 #> Hornet Sportabout 17.02  0  0    3    2  # Full store as a named list. get_correspondence_tables() #> $tc_crypt_demo #>                    mpg                        mpg_crypt #> Mazda RX4         21.0 CEA9D4F43791ACE22D90EDBF6CE405F5 #> Mazda RX4 Wag     21.0 CEA9D4F43791ACE22D90EDBF6CE405F5 #> Datsun 710        22.8 65080BE2078D5C5934F018F806E72E81 #> Hornet 4 Drive    21.4 18C5941010E3CDCB669F472DF3A1DD5E #> Hornet Sportabout 18.7 1866EA523631A281BD9431915F7C407F #>  #> $tc_persons_crypt #> # A tibble: 10 × 2 #>    email         email_crypt                      #>    <chr>         <chr>                            #>  1 a@example.com 5039481DEF58F610590FE260B994AF1A #>  2 b@example.com AA49E63CC89561CE3F7C9328486FD756 #>  3 c@example.com 5002AEF6282D0F3B1D1F24C0E1540B72 #>  4 d@example.com F7823A90F6717A115477F235B40C2C8F #>  5 e@example.com 23C53292EB01730174CDDE37EB53DA80 #>  6 f@example.com FDFAA88718AFC1AE2029177337FC982C #>  7 g@example.com BD80BBB60E8A6FF9ECCDF62249D66197 #>  8 h@example.com C19C713C407603F2656313721977BCB5 #>  9 i@example.com 05DB539F131D2FD8F659281C543D031C #> 10 j@example.com 71413F22FD5F2699715044ABEF448D78 #>   # Subset by name, preserving the requested order. get_correspondence_tables(names = "tc_crypt_demo") #> $tc_crypt_demo #>                    mpg                        mpg_crypt #> Mazda RX4         21.0 CEA9D4F43791ACE22D90EDBF6CE405F5 #> Mazda RX4 Wag     21.0 CEA9D4F43791ACE22D90EDBF6CE405F5 #> Datsun 710        22.8 65080BE2078D5C5934F018F806E72E81 #> Hornet 4 Drive    21.4 18C5941010E3CDCB669F472DF3A1DD5E #> Hornet Sportabout 18.7 1866EA523631A281BD9431915F7C407F #>

title: Profile a Data Frame — inspect
text: Returns one-row-per-column profile data frame: class, number proportion distinct values, number proportion missing values, number proportion empty strings, length range character representation, first ten unique modalities.

title: Profile a Data Frame — inspect
code: inspect(data_frame, nrow = FALSE)

title: Profile a Data Frame — inspect
text: data_frame data frame. nrow Logical scalar. TRUE, number rows data_frame emitted stderr via message() addition returned tibble. Defaults FALSE. Note parameter shadows base function base::nrow() inside function body; callers passing positional argument keep data_frame first.

title: Profile a Data Frame — inspect
text: tibble one row per column data_frame (several class(column) length > 1), holding per-column inspection metrics described .

title: Profile a Data Frame — inspect
text: Columns whose class() length > 1 (e.g. ordered factors class = c("ordered", "factor")) produce one row per class entry — mirrors historical behavior built tibble(class = class(x), ...).

title: Profile a Data Frame — inspect
code: # One row per column of CO2, with the distinct / NA / empty # counts and the first ten unique modalities. inspect(CO2) #> # A tibble: 6 × 10 #>   variables class   nb_distinct prop_distinct nb_na prop_na nb_void prop_void #>   <chr>     <chr>         <int>         <dbl> <int>   <dbl>   <int>     <dbl> #> 1 Plant     ordered          12        0.143      0       0       0         0 #> 2 Plant     factor           12        0.143      0       0       0         0 #> 3 Type      factor            2        0.0238     0       0       0         0 #> 4 Treatment factor            2        0.0238     0       0       0         0 #> 5 conc      numeric           7        0.0833     0       0       0         0 #> 6 uptake    numeric          76        0.905      0       0       0         0 #> # ℹ 2 more variables: nchars <chr>, modalities <chr>

Returns structured list (class summary.cryptR_job) carrying aggregates monitoring script typically needs: total task count, per-state counts, elapsed seconds, active workers, total rows processed (sum resolved tasks — excludes NA), output path, full per-task status dataframe.

title: Dashboard summary of an asynchronous crypt_r() job — summary.cryptR_job
text: object class summary.cryptR_job — named list elements n_tasks, counts, elapsed_sec, n_workers, total_rows, output_path, log_written, status (full cryptR_status() dataframe).

title: Dashboard summary of an asynchronous crypt_r() job — summary.cryptR_job
text: object print() method renders compact dashboard.

title: Dashboard summary of an asynchronous crypt_r() job — summary.cryptR_job
code: # \donttest{ # Build a tiny job (see ?crypt_r for a more detailed walkthrough) work_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "cryptR_summary_example") mask_dir <- file.path(work_dir, "mask") out_dir  <- file.path(work_dir, "output") int_dir  <- file.path(work_dir, "intermediate") for (d in c(mask_dir, out_dir, int_dir)) {   dir.create(d, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE) }  input_file <- system.file("extdata", "persons.csv", package = "cryptRopen") mask <- data.frame(   folder_path     = dirname(input_file),   file            = basename(input_file),   encrypted_file  = "persons_crypt.csv",   vars_to_encrypt = "email",   vars_to_remove  = NA,   to_encrypt      = "X",   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) writexl::write_xlsx(mask, file.path(mask_dir, "mask.xlsx"))  job <- crypt_r(   mask_folder_path  = mask_dir,   mask_file         = "mask.xlsx",   output_path       = out_dir,   intermediate_path = int_dir,   encryption_key    = "demo-key",   n_workers         = 1L ) job <- cryptR_collect(job) summary(job) #> <cryptR_job summary> #>   tasks        : 1 #>     running    : 0 #>     done       : 1 #>     failed     : 0 #>   workers      : 1 #>   elapsed      : 1.11 s #>   rows total   : 10 #>   output_path  : /tmp/Rtmp7huNLr/cryptR_summary_example/output #>   log_written  : TRUE  unlink(work_dir, recursive = TRUE) # }

title: cryptRopen 0.2.0
text: first public CRAN release. 0.1.x line published GitHub .

title: cryptRopen 0.2.0
text: public surface (crypt_vector(), crypt_data(), crypt_r() async companions, get_correspondence_tables(), inspect()) committed across 0.x series: existing arguments removed renamed; new arguments may added defaults.

title: cryptRopen 0.2.0
text: Title Description rewritten describe package honestly: pseudonymization via salted hash, reversible encryption. ?cryptRopen now real index — entry points, async companions, pointers two vignettes. Getting Started vignette gained “use cryptRopen” section “Algorithm choice” section. @param descriptions standardised; two factually wrong notes corrected (inspect() require data frame live globalenv(); crypt_data() accepts data frame value, expression). Examples async companions migrated \dontrun{} runnable \donttest{} blocks anchored shipped persons.csv fixture. US English spelling propagated throughout (pseudonymize, normalize, finalize, behavior, …).

title: cryptRopen 0.2.0
text: dplyr::filter_all(any_vars(...)) replaced dplyr::filter(if_any(...)) mask import step (superseded family generating soft deprecation warnings dplyr >= 1.1). inspect()’s optional row-count side effect now flows message() instead print(). crypt_data() argument-validation error messages reformulated clarity, call. = FALSE.

title: cryptRopen 0.2.0
text: DESCRIPTION gained URL, BugReports, Depends: R (>= 4.1.0), Language: en-US, Config/testthat/edition: 3. devtools removed Suggests (unused runtime). async test suite (tests/testthat/test-crypt_r_async.R) now skips CRAN via skip_on_cran(). Full coverage exercised CI every push.

title: cryptRopen 0.1.1
text: crypt_r() longer crashes mask rows empty vars_to_encrypt cell. rows now treated legitimate “copy / convert ” instructions: input file written output_path -(re-encoded requested output format), vars_to_remove applied non-empty, _crypt columns emitted, tc_*.parquet produced. recap log records success = TRUE tc_name = NA rows. Previously three engines tried build zero-length _crypt data frame failed assembly step “Can’t recycle ..1 (size 0) match ..2” (visible CSV streaming scale).

title: cryptRopen 0.1.1
text: New private helper .parse_mask_vars() centralizes str_split(",") %>% unlist() %>% str_trim() idiom three engines duplicated. also normalizes NA / "" / whitespace-/ list-internal blanks (",,b") character(0) / clean items, fixing latent edge case vars_to_remove well. .transform_stream_chunk() .process_mask_row_in_memory() now short-circuit encryption + correspondence-table construction vars_to_encrypt empty, still applying vars_to_remove.

title: cryptRopen 0.1.1
text: crypt_data() now fail-fast empty vars_to_encrypt: raises explicit error pointing user dplyr / rio plain column dropping format conversion. Previously errored , later misleading message (“indicated vars_to_encrypt must effectively variable name”). asymmetry crypt_r() intentional: crypt_r() driven hand-filled spreadsheet describing heterogeneous batch, “copy / purge ” row reasonable use case; crypt_data() direct call loaded object, calling nothing encrypt misuse silently succeeding hide mistake. Side effect: crypt_data() now trims vars_to_encrypt membership check, typo like " mpg " (surrounding whitespace) now silently accepted instead producing “indicated vars_to_encrypt…” error. historical trim happening later function already absorbed whitespace cases successful runs, aligns membership check rest pipeline.

title: cryptRopen 0.1.1
text: Two new baseline cases lock new crypt_r() empty-vars behavior: empty_vars_remove_csv (purge-CSV) empty_vars_copy_rds (verbatim copy RDS). intermediate/ directories empty contract, asserted expect_setequal() test-baseline.R. New test-parse_mask_vars.R covers helper’s edge cases (12 assertions). test-crypt_data.R adds three expect_error() fail-fast character(0), "", c(NA_character_, "  ").

title: cryptRopen 0.1.0
text: First public release milestone — closes refactor-v1 branch. public API change vs. historical 0.0.0.9000; hood, crypt_r() now non-blocking mirai orchestration, streaming engine handles large parquet / CSV inputs, correspondence tables live package-private environment, codebase restructured readability.

title: cryptRopen 0.1.0
text: refactor branch ran six-step readability audit hardening phases. API change, semantic change; tests devtools::check() stay 0/0/0 throughout. Audit 0 — retire fusen: R/*.R becomes single source truth; dev/flat_*.Rmd removed. Audit — R/cryptRopen-package.R stub consolidated @importFrom utils::globalVariables(). Audit B — redundant requireNamespace() calls removed hard Imports; function ( → function( three sites. Audit C — R/crypt_r.R (1094 lines) split seven engine-specific files: crypt_r.R, crypt_r_result.R, crypt_r_dispatcher.R, crypt_r_engine_in_memory.R, crypt_r_engine_stream_shared.R, crypt_r_engine_stream_parquet.R, crypt_r_engine_stream_csv.R. Audit D.1 — cryptic local names expanded: sm → mask_row, tf → transformed, ds → arrow_dataset, x0 → col, redundant g alias dropped. Audit D.2 — styler::style_pkg(): de-aligned = multi-line calls, normalized whitespace. Audit E — roxygen bug fixes, @family async_job, missing @return, typos, inline phase references migrated file.

what: Phase 2.C — retire assign_to_global() and clear R CMD check
title: cryptRopen 0.0.0.9000
text: R/assign_to_global.R removed along fusen flat file dev/config_fusen.yaml entry. ^tests/baseline$ added .Rbuildignore; baseline fixtures stay accessible devtools::test() via skip_if_no_baseline() excluded tarball. nanoparquet moved Imports (implicit dependency rio::export() parquet output; R CMD check ---cran exposed gap). test-baseline.R: source("cases.R") guarded file longer errors baseline directory stripped tarball. mirai::mirai() body resolves .process_mask_row() via utils::getFromNamespace() instead ::: operator — clears ::: calls package's namespace code NOTE. devtools::check() reaches 0 ERROR / 0 WARNING / 0 NOTE first time refactor.

title: cryptRopen 0.0.0.9000
text: crypt_r() validates output_path intermediate_path dispatching mirai tasks. Per-row input paths stay unchecked — per-row failure must interrupt rest. get_correspondence_tables(names = NULL): optional character vector select order returned tables. Missing keys become NULL entries. New exported cryptR_results(job): disk-oriented companion cryptR_status(). One row per filtered mask row output_file_path, live exists, worker-captured size_bytes / sha256, success, error_message. Joins cleanly cryptR_status() encrypted_file.

what: Phase 2.A — enriched job state and summary() method
title: cryptRopen 0.0.0.9000
text: cryptR_status() grows 3 7 columns: start_time, end_time, duration_sec, n_rows_processed extracted per-task payload. breaking change. New S3 method summary.cryptR_job() (exported) returns compact dashboard object; print() method renders . print.cryptR_job() gains active-workers line. Shared helper .n_workers_active() de-duplicates three open-coded mirai::status()$daemons call sites.

previous_headings: Phase 1.D — crypt_r() refactor and mirai orchestration
what: Phase 1.D.6.c — log xlsx, TC round-trip, auto-watcher
title: cryptRopen 0.0.0.9000
text: Engines return typed list(success, error_message, tc_name, tc_df, metrics) via new helper .make_row_result(). New file R/cryptR_log.R log-writing correspondence- table re-injection helpers. New file R/cryptR_watcher.R later::later() self- rescheduling watcher recap log written automatically last mirai task resolves. Graceful fallback later unavailable. cryptR_collect() now runs shared finalize pipeline (TC re- injection + log writer), idempotent watcher already fired. TC limitation 1.D.6.b lifted: get_correspondence_tables() parent process sees TCs produced mirai daemons.

previous_headings: Phase 1.D — crypt_r() refactor and mirai orchestration
title: cryptRopen 0.0.0.9000
text: crypt_r() becomes non-blocking: one mirai::mirai() per filtered mask row, results aggregated cryptR_job returned immediately. New parameter n_workers = NULL heuristic min(detectCores() - 1, n_rows, 8L) (floored 1). Existing daemons default profile reused; spawned daemons torn cryptR_collect(). .process_mask_row_in_memory() longer writes globalenv(): assign_to_global(), eval(parse(text = ...)). correspondence table lives .cryptRopen_env + disk. DESCRIPTION: job removed Imports, {parallel} added. assign_to_global() demoted @noRd (kept -op avoid breaking downstream code may still reference ).

previous_headings: Phase 1.D — crypt_r() refactor and mirai orchestration
title: cryptRopen 0.0.0.9000
text: New S3 class cryptR_job companions cryptR_status(), cryptR_wait(), cryptR_collect(), print.cryptR_job() — still wired isolation; crypt_r() touched yet. New files R/cryptR_job.R R/cryptR_job_helpers.R. DESCRIPTION: mirai added Imports, withr Suggests (test-, daemon teardown).

previous_headings: Phase 1.D — crypt_r() refactor and mirai orchestration
what: Phase 1.D.5 — baseline covers large parquet + multi-chunk streaming
title: cryptRopen 0.0.0.9000
text: New case large_parquet_multichunk (50 000 rows, chunk_size = 15000) tests/baseline/cases.R; exercises multi-iteration scanner reads. tests/baseline/generate_baseline.R: capture_one_crypt_data() now reads correspondence tables via cryptRopen::get_correspondence_tables() — fixes latent regression Phase 1.C TC names captured empty.

previous_headings: Phase 1.D — crypt_r() refactor and mirai orchestration
title: cryptRopen 0.0.0.9000
text: 1.D.4.— crypt_r(engine, chunk_size) parameters added plumbed dispatcher (routing change yet); arrow promoted Suggests Imports. Scanner + ParquetFileWriter. Correspondence table built incrementally distinct(). 1.D.4.c — CSV streaming engine via arrow::open_csv_dataset() reads; utils::write.table(append = TRUE) writes (arrow::CsvWriter universally exported). 1.D.4.d — shared helpers factored (.transform_stream_chunk(), .finalize_stream_tc(), .write_stream_inspect()); dispatcher routes parquet-/parquet-csv-/csv-streaming, mixed non-streamable endpoints -memory. rio::import() kept inspect relecture preserve baseline class() tuples CSV date columns.

previous_headings: Phase 1.D — crypt_r() refactor and mirai orchestration
what: Phase 1.D.1 → 1.D.3 — extract and freeze the in-memory engine
title: cryptRopen 0.0.0.9000
text: 1.D.1 — .process_mask_row() extracted crypt_r(); thin orchestrator + helper. 1.D.2 — .process_mask_row() becomes dispatcher; historical body moves .process_mask_row_in_memory() unchanged. 1.D.3 — three invariant test blocks lock -memory engine: inspect xlsx layout, TC parquet uniqueness, output column order. .process_mask_row_in_memory() marked @section FROZEN.

title: cryptRopen 0.0.0.9000
text: Phase 1.— crypt_vector() cleaned : helper .normalize_crypt_input() extracted, mask--hash, vapply(USE.NAMES = FALSE). Phase 1.B — inspect() cleaned : helper .inspect_column() extracted, mutate_if → vapply/lapply POSIXct columns, map_df → list_rbind(names_to = "variables"). Phase 1.C — crypt_data() decoupled globalenv(). Correspondence tables now routed .cryptRopen_env (R/private_env.R) retrievable via new exported get_correspondence_tables().

title: cryptRopen 0.0.0.9000
text: tests/baseline/ 29 cases (12 crypt_vector, 6 crypt_data, 11 crypt_r) generated generate_baseline.R compared test-baseline.R. subsequent refactors preserve byte-level equality disk outputs semantic equality -memory results.
